merged: Siyyid Ḥasan, brother of Siyyid Ḥusayn-i-‘Azíz
folds.reason: Both are the brother of the Báb's amanuensis Siyyid Ḥusayn-i-Yazdí (surnamed ‘Azíz); ‘Azíz and Yazdí are the same man, so the two are one person.
Same Najíb Páshá, Ottoman governor of Baghdád (sack of Karbilá, poisoning of Siyyid Káẓim); only transliteration of the title differs.
Both are the Báb's infant son named Aḥmad, who died 1259 A.H. before the Declaration.
Master of Ceremonies / chamberlain to the Sháh in whose Ṭihrán house Siyyid Yaḥyá (Vaḥíd) lodged — same court official.

folds.keeper: Ḥájí Siyyid Asadu’lláh (son of Ḥájí Siyyid Muḥammad-Báqir)
folds.reason: Both are the son of the late Ḥájí Siyyid Muḥammad-Báqir-i-Rashtí who declined the Mu‘tamid's gathering — same person.
Both are the faithful servant/attendant of Mullá Ḥusayn who accompanied him to Máh-Kú/Áḏhirbáyján and was martyred the same night as his master.
The Baraghání Ḥujjatu’l-Islám and the Ḥájí Mullá Taqí murdered in Qazvín are the same mujtahid (Ṭáhirih's uncle).
Same defender of Shaykh Ṭabarsí slain on leaving the fort; identical nisba and brother (Muḥammad-Ḥusayn).
Same Ṭabarsí participant summoned by Quddús; the bare entry is the Siyyid-i-Ḵhál-Dár.

folds.keeper: Mír Muḥammad-‘Alí (father of Siyyid Aḥmad of Sang-Sar)
folds.reason: Siyyid Aḥmad and Siyyid Abú-Ṭálib are brothers of the same Sang-Sar family; their father Mír Muḥammad-‘Alí is one man (devotee of Shaykh Aḥmad-i-Aḥsá’í).

folds.keeper: Mír Abu’l-Qásim (brother of Siyyid Aḥmad of Sang-Sar)
merged: Mír Abu’l-Qásim, brother of Siyyid Abú-Ṭálib-i-Sang-Sárí
folds.reason: Siyyid Aḥmad and Siyyid Abú-Ṭálib are brothers in the same Sang-Sar family; their brother Mír Abu’l-Qásim, martyred the night Mullá Ḥusayn fell, is one man.
Both are the leading mujtahid of Sárí in whose home Quddús was confined and who, with seven ‘ulamás, put Quddús to death.
Same Mázindarán/Ṭabarsí martyr slain at Bárfurúsh after the fort was abandoned.

folds.keeper: Mullá Taqí (uncle and father-in-law of Ṭáhirih)
folds.reason: Ṭáhirih's uncle/father-in-law Mullá Taqí (Baraghání) is the same man whose murder in Qazvín is repeatedly referenced.
Both describe the single man Ṣafar whose brother S̱ha‘bán fell in the war.
Both describe the single man S̱ha‘bán, brother of Ṣafar, who perished in the war.
Same man: elder brother of Zaynu’l-‘Ábidín Khán, father of Áqá Ján, who perished in the Zanján operations.
